old_claim.note: Bronze Casting Molds provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because the broad node is not restricted to cast bronze bearings.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica Wheel anchors wheeled vehicles in the fourth millennium BCE; the node description permits greased wooden, leather, or metal bearing surfaces.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Early cart axle bearings are friction-reducing surfaces for wheeled vehicles and need not be bronze castings.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Wooden or leather bearing surfaces can support the broad cart-bearing node before bronze casting molds.

ontology_before: Cart Axle Bearings treated Bronze Casting Molds as an enabling prerequisite.
ontology_after: Cart Axle Bearings remains tied to wheeled vehicles and leather/wood material capability, not bronze casting.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Cart Axle Bearings must not directly depend on Bronze Casting Molds unless narrowed to cast bronze axle bearings.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to cast bronze axle bearings.
A source shows the earliest cart bearings were specifically cast bronze.
Wheeled-vehicle chronology is revised later than bronze mold chronology.
short_reason: Broad cart bearing surfaces do not require bronze casting molds.
